retrieval.query_summary: Analysis of Crash 1000 Index for a bearish continuation trade based on HTF alignment and LTF structural break.

factors.name: Macro bias alignment
factors.notes: Macro environment is neutral and non-obstructive for this synthetic index.

factors.name: HTF structure aligned
factors.notes: Setup direction (SHORT) aligns with the dominant bearish trend on D1, H12, H8, H6, and H4 timeframes.

factors.name: MTF BOS or ChoCH confirmed
factors.notes: M30, M15, and M5 have all confirmed bearish structure, indicating the H1 pullback is over and trend is resuming down.

factors.name: Valid Structural Entry Support
factors.notes: A valid M5 Bearish Order Block is present at the entry zone [5621.006, 5622.62].

factors.name: Liquidity sweep into entry zone
factors.notes: The candidate confirms a sweep of Buy-Side Liquidity (BSL) occurred prior to the Break of Market Structure (BMS).

factors.name: COT alignment
factors.notes: COT data was not available for this analysis cycle.

factors.name: Wyckoff phase supports direction
factors.notes: No clear Wyckoff schematic was identified in the provided data.

factors.name: No high-impact news
factors.notes: The economic calendar is clear of any high-impact events within the next 30 minutes.

factors.name: Minimum R:R achievable
factors.notes: The R:R to the final structural target (TP3) is approximately 1:69, far exceeding the 1:3 minimum for Intraday style.

cot_signal.summary: Data not available due to collection error.

evidence.content_preview: All trades must originate from one or more of the following frameworks: SMC: SH+BMS+RTO

raw_response.explainable_reasoning: The analysis identifies a high-probability short opportunity on the Crash 1000 Index, aligning with a strong bearish trend on the higher timeframes (D1-H4). While H1 has been in a bullish pullback, the lower timeframes (M30, M15, M5) have recently broken structure to the downside, signaling a likely end to the pullback and a resumption of the dominant bearish order flow. A live M5 SMC pattern, specifically a SH+BMS+FVG+RTO (Stop Hunt, Break of Market Structure, Fair Value Gap, Return to Order Block), has been identified. The system will place a limit order at the M5 Bearish Order Block between 5621.006 and 5622.62. The stop loss is placed structurally above this zone at 5622.7814. Take profit targets are set at sequential liquidity pools on the M5, M15, and H1 timeframes, offering an excellent R:R ratio of approximately 1:69 to the final target. The setup has a confluence score of 7/10, earning it an 'A' grade.

htf_bias.notes: The overall trend is bearish, confirmed by alignment from D1 down to H4. The W1 and H1 timeframes are showing counter-trend pullbacks, but the dominant institutional order flow remains bearish.

mtf_bias.notes: The H1 bullish pullback appears to be failing as the LTFs (M30, M15, M5) have all broken structure to the downside, realigning with the HTF bearish trend. This indicates a potential continuation of the primary bearish move.

evidence.content_preview: SL must be structural — placed beyond the invalidation level of the setup (beyond OB, beyond QML, beyond swing high/low)

stop_loss.reason: Placed above the M5 Swing High and the invalidation level of the Bearish Order Block.
